label: The Sealed Burden
description: A desperate patron hires you to carry a sealed package across hostile lands—no questions, no opening. As the road darkens and whispers gather, you must decide what “safe delivery” truly means.
plot: Opening hook: even untouched, the package occasionally weeps cold ash from its seams and makes nearby flames burn a sickly blue. A desperate patron hires the player to deliver a sealed package to a far-off fortress-library and insists it must remain unopened, promising life-changing pay and quiet favors. Along the road, an organized hunter-network led by a soft-spoken cult agent closes in, using bribed checkpoints, staged “rescues,” and cursed hounds that track the package’s faint spiritual leak. The player learns the package is not merely contraband but a binding vessel for a starving presence that can be used to purge a spreading blight—or unleash it as a controllable weapon—depending on who performs the final rite. If the seal is broken, the presence lashes out: shadows warp, memories bleed into the air, and the opener becomes spiritually “tagged,” drawing more horrors and weakening nearby wards. At the fortress-library, an exhausted archivist-priest admits the delivery is part of a grim bargain: to save many, someone must pay a personal cost, and the patron’s story omits who is meant to suffer. The climax forces a choice under pressure—deliver, destroy, or bargain—while deciding what to do about the cult agent’s offer of “order” bought with blood.

characters.description: A well-dressed, sleepless patron with ink-stained fingers and a practiced, pleading smile; their hands tremble when they mention the seal.
characters.publicFace: A charitable sponsor funding “relief efforts” and offering honest work to capable adventurers.
characters.hiddenTruth: They arranged the delivery to force a final ritual at the destination, hoping the player will become the convenient sacrifice if things go wrong.
characters.locationHint: a crowded market district where hired blades and couriers gather
characters.matchHint: desperate patron posing as a charitable sponsor

characters.description: A scarred road-guide with a cracked voice and sharp eyes who reads tracks like scripture and never sleeps near open fires.
characters.publicFace: A helpful guide who knows safe routes, smugglers’ signals, and which shrines still hold power.
characters.hiddenTruth: They once helped move similar sealed cargo; they feel guilty and are trying to steer the player toward the least-harmful outcome without admitting their role.
characters.locationHint: an old roadside tavern used by caravans and mercenaries
characters.matchHint: scarred road-guide hiding guilt from past courier work

characters.description: A calm, soft-spoken organizer in practical travel gear whose politeness never reaches their eyes; they carry ritual thread and keepsake bones.
characters.publicFace: A legitimate security coordinator offering protection, escorts, and “official” paperwork to pass checkpoints.
characters.hiddenTruth: They lead a cult cell that wants the bound presence for controlled purges and political leverage, and they will happily burn villages to prevent the package being destroyed.
characters.locationHint: a border checkpoint where guards accept bribes and favors
characters.matchHint: soft-spoken cult agent posing as a security coordinator

characters.description: An exhausted keeper of records with ash-gray hair and warding scars along the forearms; they speak like someone used to confessions.
characters.publicFace: A strict custodian of a fortress-library who believes containment and sacrifice are sometimes necessary.
characters.hiddenTruth: They intend to subvert the planned rite if shown a viable alternative, but fear the consequences of defying their order and the wards failing.
characters.locationHint: a remote fortress-library dedicated to curses and containment
characters.matchHint: exhausted archivist-priest torn between duty and mercy

places.description: A muddy sprawl of stalls and candlelit arcades where minor charms are sold beside stolen relics; watchmen look away for coin.
places.storyRole: Where the job is offered and where the first subtle signs of pursuit and corruption appear.
places.matchHint: crowded market district where couriers and smugglers trade favors

places.description: A narrow, wind-scoured route lined with half-buried ward-stones and broken shrines; the air tastes metallic at dusk.
places.storyRole: A chokepoint for ambushes and the first major test of whether the player will keep the package sealed.
places.matchHint: wind-scoured mountain pass lined with failing ward-stones

places.description: A fortress of black stone and chained bells, its halls packed with censored tomes and sealed reliquaries; wards hum like distant insects.
places.storyRole: The destination and moral crucible where the package’s purpose is revealed and the final decision is made.
places.matchHint: remote fortress-library studying curses and containment

turningPoints.involvedCharacter: desperate patron posing as a charitable sponsor
essentialInformation: the player learns they are being hired to deliver a sealed package to a far-off destination
the player learns the package must be kept safe and unopened, and tampering is strongly warned against
the player learns someone else is likely searching for the package, making the route dangerous

turningPoints.involvedCharacter: scarred road-guide hiding guilt from past courier work
essentialInformation: the player learns the package can be tracked by unnatural means, suggesting it leaks a faint presence
the player learns pursuit is organized rather than random banditry, implying a network with resources

turningPoints.involvedCharacter: soft-spoken cult agent posing as a security coordinator
essentialInformation: the player learns opening or cracking the seal causes a strange event that marks the opener and worsens nearby distortions
the player learns the package contains a bound presence that can be used for cleansing or weaponization depending on the final rite
the player learns at least one faction prefers the player alive only if they remain controllable

turningPoints.involvedCharacter: exhausted archivist-priest torn between duty and mercy
essentialInformation: the player learns the destination’s planned ritual requires a personal cost and someone is meant to bear it
the player learns the original hiring story omitted who benefits and who pays, forcing a moral choice under time pressure

turningPoints.involvedCharacter: soft-spoken cult agent posing as a security coordinator
essentialInformation: the player must choose what ultimately happens to the package: deliver it, destroy it, or surrender it as part of a bargain
the player learns the bound presence cannot be safely ignored—indecision risks a breach or takeover

turningPoints.involvedCharacter: exhausted archivist-priest torn between duty and mercy
essentialInformation: the player learns their choice reshapes local safety and future persecution: containment, uncontrolled spread, or authoritarian “purges”
the player learns whether they carry ongoing spiritual consequences depends on if the seal was broken and what bargain was made

goals.description: Accept custody of the sealed package and the terms of delivery.
Keep the package sealed while you travel, resisting curiosity, coercion, and “helpful” offers.
Survive the road and reach the far-off destination with the package still in your possession.
Make a final decision: deliver the package, destroy it, or surrender it to the pursuers.
Remove the pursuing leader as an immediate threat—by defeating them or binding them with a bargain.
Decide whether to accept the required cost to stabilize what you’ve set in motion.

possibleFlags.triggerDescription: Set when the player agrees to take custody of the sealed package.
Set when the player opens the package or breaks the seal enough to expose its contents.
Set when the player reaches the destination without ever opening or significantly breaking the seal.
Set when the player arrives at the remote destination with the package still in their possession.
Set when the player hands the package to the intended recipient or places it into the destination’s custody.
Set when the player successfully destroys the package and its binding vessel in a way that prevents simple recovery.
Set when the player gives the package to the pursuing faction or relinquishes it under coercion.
Set when the player defeats the main pursuing leader (by combat, exposure, or removing their operational control).
Set when the player makes a binding bargain with a major faction regarding the package’s use or their own marked state.
Set when the player accepts a personal or moral cost to stabilize the outcome (sacrifice, confession, binding oath, or equivalent).
Set when the player refuses the demanded cost despite understanding the consequences.

endingCards.title: Delivered Into Darkness
endingCards.text: The wards take the package—along with the weight of whatever bargain made that possible. The world grows quieter for now, but you wonder who the silence is truly serving.

endingCards.title: Ashes and Afterimages
endingCards.text: You deny every faction their prize, but the act leaves scars on land and spirit. Some call you a savior; others swear you doomed them without knowing it.

endingCards.title: The Price of Convenience
endingCards.text: The pursuers take the package and the future tightens like a noose. Order returns in brutal strokes, and your name becomes useful leverage.

endingCards.title: A Covenant Written in Blood
endingCards.text: You survive by agreement, not victory. The pact protects you—for as long as you keep paying what it demands.

endingCards.title: The Road Remembers
endingCards.text: In the weeks after, rumors follow you: blue-burning fires, missing prayers, and a sealed thing that made honest people choose dishonesty. Whatever you did, something out there learned your shape.
